diagrams.description: The timeline traces the evolution of Sociological Theory. It begins with Enlightenment precursors (Montesquieu’s 1748 The Spirit of Laws, Smith’s 1776 Wealth of Nations). The classical period (1838–1920s) saw Comte’s positivism, Marx’s historical materialism (1848), Durkheim’s rules and suicide (1893‑1897), and Weber’s interpretive sociology (1904‑1905). Mid‑20th century brought structural functionalism (Parsons, 1937) and revived conflict theory (Mills, 1959). The 1960s‑70s introduced symbolic interactionism (Goffman, 1959), feminist theory, and poststructuralism (Foucault). Late 20th century saw Bourdieu’s practice theory (1972), globalization (Wallerstein), and intersectionality (Crenshaw, 1989). Contemporary developments include actor‑network theory (Latour) and digital sociology.
